titleStmt.title: SII 19.241: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 241. (A.R. No. 321 of 1904.) KUḌUMIYĀMALAI, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE. ON THE NORTH FACE OF THE NORTH PILLAR OF THE ROCK-CUT SHRINE, MĒLAIKKŌYIL TEMPLE.

summary.p: This records a gift of 7(1/2) kaḻañju of gold (tuḷaippoṉ) for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumūlaṭṭānattu-Perumānaḍigal at Tirunalakkuṟṟam in Kuṉṟiyūrnāḍu by Naṅgai-Na[la]dēviyar wife of Śembiyaṉ Irukkuvēḷār. The latter is evidently identical with the Koḍumbāḷūr chief Pūdi (Bhūti) Parāntaka son of Bhūti Vikramakēśari, who under king Āditya Karīkāla overcame Vīra-Pāṇḍya (vide M.E.R. for 1908, paras. 88 and 91 and S.I.I. Vol. III, No. 139). This may be assigned to the reign of Uttama Chōḷa. (No. 52, Pud. Ins. Texts.)

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . kopparake
caripaṉmaṟku yāṇṭu pattā
vatu Ivvāṇṭu kuṉṟiyūrnā
ṭṭut tirunalakkuṉṟattut ti
rumulaṭṭāṉattup perumā
ṉaṭikaḷukkuc cempiyaṉ Iru
kkuveḷār teviyār naṅkai
nalteviyār nontāviḷa
kkoṉṟukku vaitta tuḷaippo
ṉ Eḻukaḻañcarai Itu paṉmā
heśvararakṣai ....
Digital edition of SII 19.241 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
